zotero.abstractNote: Mental health problems are highly prevalent in children and adolescents. Schools are important settings for mental health promotion and prevention. However, little is known about school-related predictors of mental health problems in students in hospital schools compared to regular schools. In this cross-sectional study, we surveyed 1,056 students (Mage = 13.6 years, SD = 2.3) from hospital schools (n = 156) and regular schools (n = 900) in Germany. We assessed mental health problems (SDQ), school climate, teacher-student relationship, and school-related stress. Students in hospital schools reported significantly higher levels of mental health problems than students in regular schools. In both groups, a positive teacher-student relationship and a positive school climate were associated with fewer mental health problems, while school-related stress was associated with more mental health problems. The results highlight the importance of school-related factors for the mental health of students in both settings and suggest that interventions should focus on improving the school climate and teacher-student relationships.
